label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question says today is the second day of the third month of 1966. So I need to figure out what the date will be tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY format. First, let's break down the given information. The third month of the year is March. So if today is the second day of March, that would be March 2nd, 1966. So today's date is 03/02/1966. Then, tomorrow would be the next day, which is March 3rd, 1966. So the date tomorrow would be 03/03/1966. Let me check the options given. Option A is 03/03/1966. That seems right. But let me double-check to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Wait, the third month is March. So March 2nd, adding one day would be March 3rd. So the month stays the same, the day increments by one. So yes, 03/03/1966. Let me check if there's any trick here. For example, is there a possibility that the second day of the third month refers to something else? Well, months are typically numbered 1-12, with March being the third month (03). So the second day of March is the 2nd. So the next day is the 3rd. Therefore, the answer should be option A, 03/03/1966. Let me also check the other options just in case. Option C is 03/04/1966, which would be two days after today. Option E is 03/02/1966, which is today's date. Option D is 02/28/1966, which would be the last day of February, but since we're in March already, that doesn't make sense.
